# Margin

**Object**

The object for defining the space above and below a component.

## Declaration

```swift
object Margin
```

## Discussion

Use the `margin` object to specify top and bottom margins to designate space above and below a component. For information on the units, see [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNews/specifying-measurements-for-components].

You can use this object in [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ComponentLayout] and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/AutoPlacementLayout].

### Example

```json
{
  "components": [
    {
      "role": "header",
      "layout": "headerLayout",
      "components": [
        {
          "role": "title",
          "text": "Article Title"
        }
      ]
    }
  ],
  "componentLayouts": {
    "headerLayout": {
      "margin": {
        "top": 50,
        "bottom": 50
      }
    }
  }
}
```

## Article Layout

- **Planning the Layout for Your Article**: Define a layout that supports the look you want for your article.
- **Positioning the Content in Your Article**: Align article components with columns in your layout.
- **Wrapping Text Around a Component**: Define the layout of a text component to wrap around another component.
- **Layout**: The object for defining columns, gutters, and margins for your article’s designed width.
- **ComponentLayout**: The object for defining the positioning for a specific component within the article’s column system.
- **Anchor**: The object for anchoring one component to another component in your article’s layout.
- **AutoPlacementLayout**: The object for defining the margin above and below advertising components.
- **AdvertisingLayout**: The object for defining the margin above and below advertising components.
